title: centromadia parryi subsp. rudis
intro: Centromadia parryi subsp. rudis, also known as Island Yarrow or Rare Yarrow, is a perennial herb belonging to the sunflower family. Native to the Channel Islands off the coast of California, it has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Centromadia parryi subsp. rudis include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
